comments.body: I used the PI with other languages. There you can choose to use a pin for input or output, and with input you can use pull-up or pull-down resistors so an unconnected pin would show low or high input. I guess PI4J should offer similar options. Ah, they have a nice example: pi4j.com/documentation/io-examples/digital-input

answers.body:
try using the provisionDigitalMultipurposePin method to set first mode INPUT in order to read the state, and then switch to OUTPUT mode to change the pin state.

GpioController gpio = GpioFactory.getInstance();
GpioPinDigitalMultipurpose led = gpio.provisionDigitalMultipurposePin(RaspiPin.GPIO_07, PinMode.DIGITAL_INPUT);

// read state

led.setMode(PinMode.DIGITAL_OUTPUT);

// write state

title: Raspberry PI, Java and Pi4J Controlling GPIO can't get current PinState
body:
I'm currently working on a little program which should check the current state of an explicit GPIO pin and then toggle it. For this I'm using Java and PI4J. When my program starts, the LED is turned off. But as soon as I get it as a variable it automatically sets the state to HIGH. Dos anyone have an idea how to avoid this? my code so far:

final GpioController gpio = GpioFactory.getInstance();
GpioPinDigitalOutput led =gpio.provisionDigitalMultipurposePin(RaspiPin.GPIO_07,PinMode.DIGITAL_OUTPUT);
            

PinState ledStatus = led.getState();
if (ledStatus.isHigh())
{
    led.setShutdownOptions(true, PinState.LOW);
    System.out.println("Set shutdownOption LOW");
}
else
{
    led.setShutdownOptions(true, PinState.HIGH);
    System.out.println("Set shutdownOption HIGH");
} 

if(ledStatus.isHigh())
{
    System.out.println("LEDS aus...");
}
else 
{
    System.out.println("LEDS an...");
}
led.toggle();

This Works quite fine. But as I said, before I start the program... the LED is off! as soon as I get to this line:

GpioPinDigitalOutput led =gpio.provisionDigitalMultipurposePin(RaspiPin.GPIO_07,PinMode.DIGITAL_OUTPUT);

The LED is ON! I need to figure out a way to get the state before the app is running, change it and then exit my program with the LED toggled.

Thanks for your help
